reasoning: The 10% top-off water change has settled cleanly — all parameters remain within target and TDS has risen naturally due to water loss and evaporation, not accumulation. Colony is stable 23 days post-introduction with acclimation mostly behind them.

actions.note: Next scheduled test; nitrate still at 80ppm yesterday—cycle is active and stable.
Behavior, coloration, feeding response. Any signs of lethargy or stress (shrimp are resilient post-acclimation, but worth monitoring after recent loss).
Last photo was 17hr ago; tank view would confirm shrimp visibility and duckweed status for daily log.

ph.note: 0.5-point drop in 1 hour post top-off; likely acidification from fresh water interaction with substrate or biofilm, not a crisis, but warrants observation

reasoning: The pH drop from 6.83 to 6.32 in one hour post-water-change is notable but explainable — top-off water interacting with an actively cycling substrate can release organic acids; TDS actually climbed (+21ppm over 24hr) despite the dilution, suggesting bioload is building. This is normal for day 36 in a young cycle.

actions.note: pH will likely stabilize within 2–4 hours; re-test in afternoon to confirm recovery
Re-run full kit (ammonia, nitrite, nitrate, pH) by afternoon to confirm pH rebound and track ongoing cycle

ph.note: Sharp 0.44 drop in 1 hour post-water-change; current reading 0.15 below target floor but within acclimation tolerance. Stabilization expected as fresh water equilibrates.

reasoning: The pH drop is a direct consequence of the 10% top-off—fresh water typically shifts the tank downward before equilibrating. This is noise, not signal. All other parameters remain solid; shrimp show no distress behavior in recent photos, and ammonia/nitrite remain zero.

actions.note: Recheck pH in 2-3 hours to confirm equilibration; current reading is post-change artifact.
